focused-tests.title: Focused test remediation template
focused-tests.why: Repeated run history shows focused test failures usually come from stale expectations, overspecified assertions, or missing adjacent regression coverage rather than the core feature direction being wrong.
checklist: Confirm the failing assertion still matches the requested user-visible behavior instead of an implementation detail.
If the requested change intentionally altered copy or rendering output, update the affected snapshots or goldens instead of forcing code back toward stale expectations.
Add or adjust the smallest adjacent regression test that proves the real requested behavior after the fix.
Record the exact failing command and the smallest green retry command in the evidence manifest.
focused-tests.approval_prompt: Approve keeping the focused-tests remediation template as a default GEPA retry aid.

app-tests.title: Application test-suite remediation template
app-tests.why: Recoverable reflections show app-wide test failures often surface package-local mocks, contract drift, or stale fixtures outside the focused feature slice.
checklist: Treat app-wide failures as contract-checking evidence before assuming the feature implementation is wrong.
If interfaces, backends, or shared state changed, run the full relevant suite early instead of waiting until the end of the run.
Update package-local mocks, fixtures, or shared helpers together with the feature change so the suite reflects the new contract.
Capture which failure was feature-adjacent versus infrastructure-adjacent to keep the retry plan bounded.
app-tests.approval_prompt: Approve keeping the app-tests remediation template as a default GEPA retry aid.

app-package-tests.title: Package-level test remediation template
app-package-tests.why: Package-level failures usually indicate a narrow contract or snapshot expectation drift that should be repaired without exploding the retry scope.
checklist: Check whether the package failure is protecting a user-visible contract such as hints, status text, or layout at constrained widths.
Refresh package-local snapshots or fixtures when the requested user-visible change made the old expectation obsolete.
Avoid widening immediately to a full-suite retry unless the package failure indicates a shared interface change.
Document the preserved contract explicitly in the retry note so the next attempt stays focused.
app-package-tests.approval_prompt: Approve keeping the app-package-tests remediation template as a default GEPA retry aid.

diff-check.title: Diff-check remediation template
diff-check.why: Diff-check failures in terminal UIs often come from intentional whitespace, frame, or snapshot fidelity rather than a functional regression.
checklist: Verify whether the diff is protecting terminal fidelity such as trailing spaces, frame borders, or line wrapping rather than semantic behavior.
Do not normalize snapshot output just to satisfy the diff when the formatting is part of the rendered contract.
Update the snapshot only after confirming the new output matches the requested UI behavior and terminal size expectations.
Capture the exact before/after artifact pair that justified the snapshot change.
diff-check.approval_prompt: Approve keeping the diff-check remediation template as a default GEPA retry aid.

input-routing-safety.title: Input-routing safety remediation template
input-routing-safety.why: Recovered shortcut and alias failures show that text-entry regressions usually come from routing changes that were validated on the trigger path but not on compose, prompt, and editor surfaces.
checklist: Prove the change on each affected text-entry surface, not just on the shortcut trigger path that motivated the change.
Record whether the typed literal stayed in the field and whether any alias or hotkey fired unexpectedly.
Capture one transcript or screenshot artifact per exercised surface so future sessions can replay the same routing proof.
Keep alias expansion scoped away from text-entry contexts unless the product truth explicitly says the field should intercept that input.
input-routing-safety.approval_prompt: Approve keeping the input-routing safety remediation template as a default GEPA retry aid.
